output.code:
# Let's open and read the text file to analyze the content and compute indices.
path = "/mnt/data/file-7hSCoHgPgUtcTqNcqqGsby-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:1000]

# Let's programmatically find quoted segments and inspect them.
text = open("/mnt/data/file-7hSCoHgPgUtcTqNcqqGsby-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

# Find all double-quoted segments
segments = []
i = 0
while True:
    start_quote = text.find('"', i)
    if start_quote == -1:
        break
    end_quote = text.find('"', start_quote+1)
    if end_quote == -1:
        # no closing quote; take till end as a segment?
        segments.append((start_quote, len(text)))
        break
    segments.append((start_quote, end_quote))
    i = end_quote + 1

segments, len(segments)

import string

# define set of punctuation to trim at edges per instruction
edge_punct = set(list('\'"«»()[]،؛:.,!?…'))  # include apostrophe? They said quotes ' " so yes
# We'll also include whitespace to strip
def trim_edges(text, start, end):
    # trim leading spaces and listed punctuation
    i = start
    while i < end and (text[i].isspace() or text[i] in edge_punct):
        i += 1
    j = end - 1
    while j >= i and (text[j].isspace() or text[j] in edge_punct):
        j -= 1
    # return the trimmed [i, j+1)
    return i, j+1

spans = []
for (qstart, qend) in segments:
    inner_start = qstart+1
    inner_end = qend
    if qend == len(text):  # if no closing quote, inner_end is end
        inner_end = len(text)
    tstart, tend = trim_edges(text, inner_start, inner_end)
    snippet = text[tstart:tend]
    spans.append((tstart, tend, snippet[:100] + ('...' if len(snippet)>100 else ''), snippet[-50:]))
spans

for (start, end, _, _) in spans:
    # show surrounding characters
    bef = text[start-1] if start > 0 else ''
    aft = text[end] if end < len(text) else ''
    print(start, end)
    print("Before:", repr(bef), "First char:", repr(text[start]), "Last char:", repr(text[end-1]), "After:", repr(aft))

first_start, first_end = spans[0][0], spans[0][1]
second_start, second_end = spans[1][0], spans[1][1]

first_text = text[first_start:first_end]
second_text = text[second_start:second_end]

len(first_text), first_text[-100:], len(second_text), second_text[:100]

# Check presence of newline in spans
print("\\n" in first_text, "\\n" in second_text)
